title: slop-mop response envelope
description: The invariant outer frame emitted by every sm verb in --format json. The data block is verb-specific and refined by a per-verb data schema; everything else is identical across commands so an agent can predict response shape before running any command.

schema.description: Schema identifier. Consumers assert on this to detect shape drift.

command.description: The verb that produced this response, e.g. swab, status, doctor.

status.description: Outcome class. ok=success condition held; fail=ran and found blocking problems; error=could not complete; info=observatory verb with no pass/fail.

exit_code.description: Mirrors the process exit code so a consumer reading captured stdout need not also inspect the shell status.

data.description: Verb-specific payload. Refined by the per-verb data schema referenced from sm capabilities.

next_steps.description: Ordered, machine-actionable follow-ups. Each carries a command an agent can run without parsing prose.

diagnostics.description: Execution-context warnings (missing tool, no PR detected, cached results). Distinct from gate findings, which live in data.

command.description: Exact command to run, or null when the step is not a single command.

code.description: Stable machine identifier for the condition.

suggested_command.description: Optional command that addresses the condition.
